gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (204/ 8)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (204/ 8)

parts.text: عَلِيِّ بْنِ الْحُسَيْنِ ( صلوات الله عليه )

text: قَالَ مَا أُحِبُّ أَنَّ لِي بِذُلِّ نَفْسِي حُمْرَ النَّعَمِ وَ مَا تَجَرَّعْتُ مِنْ جُرْعَةٍ أَحَبَّ إِلَيَّ مِنْ جُرْعَةِ غَيْظٍ لَا أُكَافِي بِهَا صَاحِبَهَا .

en.hubeali: From him, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Khallad, from Al Sumaly,

(It has been narrated) from Ali<sup>asws</sup> Bin Al-Husayn<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘I<sup>asws</sup> do not love it if I<sup>asws</sup> were to humiliate myself<sup>asws</sup> for the Bounties, and there is no potion from the potions to be swallowed, more beloved to me<sup>asws</sup> than the potion of anger, not seeking retribution by it from its agitator’.
en.sarwar: 12. It is narrated from him (narrator of the Hadith above) from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Khallad from al-Thumali from Ali ibn al-Husayn (a.s.) who has said the following: “I do not love to have the most noble of the bounties at the cost of humiliation to my soul. I have not swallowed any drop more beloved to me than the drop of anger for which I did not compensate from the one who caused it (my anger).”
